actionLibraryList.title: Forearm Plank Cable Pull
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Set the machine mount to knee height and attach the handle to the mount.
2.Set up in a high plank with your feet away from the machine and your hands near the edge of the base.
3.Grab the handle with one hand, keeping your body tight and your eyes looking down.
4.Pull the handle towards you, bringing your elbow towards your hip.
5.The goal is to keep your body stabilized through your core and perform controlled movement.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the handle to a mid-low position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Hold a high plank position and grasp the handle in your hand.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Pull the handle backward while keeping your body stable.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Make sure you tuck your elbow tight to your side.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you pull the handle backward and inhale as you release.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Brace your core throughout the movement and squeeze your lats as you pull the handle backward.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Grab the handle with one hand, keeping your body tight and eyes down.\n2.Keep your body stabilized through your core.\n3.The goal is to control the movement.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750930051905.jpg"},{"context":"Pull the handle backward until your elbow is close to your torso.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750930055901.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale as you pull the handle backward and inhale as you release.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Brace your core throughout the movement and squeeze your lats as you pull the handle backward.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Make sure you tuck your elbow tight to your side.

actionLibraryList.title: Tricep Rope Goblet Squat
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Stand on the side of the machine, holding the ankle rope with both hands.
2.Exhale as you squat; keep your thighs in line with your knees, and make sure your knees are pushed out.
3.Return to the standing position with your glutes and legs engaged.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Adjust the clip to one side of the footboard.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Keep your back straight and core engaged.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Ensure the knees are aligned with the direction of the toes.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Inhale as you squat down, exhale as you stand up.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Feel significant activation in the front of the thighs and glutes.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750231957013.jpg","context":"1.Hold the rope handles at chest level with both hands.\n2.Stand with feet shoulder-width apart or slightly wider."},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1750231960569.jpg","context":"1.Make sure your knees track in line with your toes.\n2.Squat down until your thighs are parallel to the ground or slightly lower."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Inhale as you squat, exhale as you stand.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Keep your torso stable, avoiding leaning forward or sagging.

actionLibraryList.title: Dual-Handle Calf Raise
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the grips to ground level.
2.Stand in a neutral position, holding the handle with both hands. Brace your core and keep your body stable.                                                                                                                                                       3.Perform a calf raise. Fully squeeze your calf muscles, and then return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Stand in a neutral position, holding the handle with both hands.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Rise up onto your toes, and fully squeeze the calf muscle group.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Exhale as you go up, inhale as you go down.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Keep your shoulders down and hold the handle with both hands.\n2.Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing forward.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934516077.jpg"},{"context":"1.Perform a calf raise. Fully squeeze your calf muscles, and then return to the starting position.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748934520001.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale on the way up, inhale on the way down.

actionLibraryList.title: Seated Dual-Handle Arnold Press
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Sit on the bench with your back straight and your core engaged.
2.Grip the handles with both hands and lift them up to about shoulder level, palms facing you.
3.As you press upward, rotate your wrists so your palms face forward, until your arms are naturally extended.
4.Slowly lower the handles back down to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Anchor the pulleys to the bottom with two handles attached.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Sit on the bench with a neutral posture.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: As your arms are fully extended overhead, rotate your wrists inward.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Don't hunch your shoulders or lock your elbows. Keep your forearms vertical to the floor.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Breathe naturally. Exhale as you press the handles overhead, inhale, then slowly return to the starting position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: Engage your triceps as you press and rotate the handles until your palms face forward. Hold the contraction at the top for one to two seconds.
actionLibraryList.showDetails:
[{"context":"1.Let the straps drop down, hold the handles with your palms facing yourself.
2.Keep your forearms vertical to the ground.
3.Keep your lower back and torso straight.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748154036280.jpg"},{"context":"1.Rotate the handles and press them up to both sides of your head, with your palms facing forward.
2.Extend your arms as straight as possible, but do not lock your elbows.
3.Keep your waist and back straight.","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1748154042552.jpg"}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Exhale while pressing up, inhale while lowering.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: Engage the deltoids and pause at peak contraction for 1–2 seconds.

actionLibraryList.title: Seated Incline Cable Reverse Crunch
actionLibraryList.context:
1.Adjust the machine attachment mounts to the 9th position.
2.Place the inclined bench on the machine base, facing the screen. 
3.Sit on the bench facing the screen.
4.Engage your abs to drive your hips upward.
5.Use diaphragmatic breathing and exhale during exertion.
6.Keep your core engaged and pulse at 65% of your maximum effort.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice1Txt: Set the machine to secure the mounts at the 9th position.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice2Txt: Place the incline bench on the machine base, facing the screen. Secure the ankle strap to the mounting bracket.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice3Txt: Sit on the bench facing the screen.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice4Txt: Engage your abs to drive your hips upward.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice5Txt: Breathe diaphragmatically and exhale during the exertion phase.
actionLibraryList.guideVoice6Txt: This exercise primarily engages your abdominal muscles and hip flexors.
actionLibraryList.showDetails: [{"context":"1.Place the slant bench lengthwise on the footplate.\n2.Sit on the bench facing the screen.\n","img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749204308428.jpg"},{"img":"https://d1rwzqjevxdih5.cloudfront.net/1749204313098.jpg","context":"1.Lift your hips upward using your abs."}]
actionLibraryList.breathingRate: Breathe diaphragmatically and exhale during the exertion phase.
actionLibraryList.motionFeeling: This exercise primarily engages your abdominal muscles and hip flexors.
actionLibraryList.errorCorrection: Engage your abs to drive your hips upward.
